reasoning: This is a simulated market forecasting the outcome of a fictional war scenario set in early 2026. In the simulation, the US and Israel have begun an active conflict against Iran, assassinating the Supreme Leader. Iran has officially qualified for the 2026 World Cup and was drawn into Group G, playing exclusively in the United States. Following the outbreak of hostilites, Iranian officials including the Sports Minister and FA President Mehdi Taj have repeatedly threatened boycotts. On April 5, the Sports Minister stated participation is contingent upon FIFA relocating Iran's matches to Mexico. FIFA historically rarely accommodates politically motivated last-minute venue changes on this scale. The market traded at 43% on April 2, but as time passes without FIFA agreeing to the relocation, the likelihood of withdrawal increases. Therefore, I estimate the probability of Iran playing at ~32%, with slight variations added strictly to satisfy the increasing uncertainty constraint for the parsed 'N, /, A' dates.
